titleStmt.title: SII 26.317: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 317. (A.R. No. 299 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai and Īḻam and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records a gift of two sandhi lamps to the same temple as occurs in No. 316, etc., above, by a certain Śendāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ, son of Kumāraṉ Śāttaṉ Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyāṉ of Vēlūr in Damaṉūr-nāḍu, in Ūttukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam. He also endowed a sum of 4 paḻaṅkāśu for the purpose. The money was accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Uḍaiyappiḷḷai alias Nittakalyāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ alias son of Kāñcikkuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Dēvappiriyaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra] who had right of worship in the temple and who had agreed to carry out the burning of the lamps.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvaṉaccakravarttikaḷ maturaiyum Īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭit talaiyuṅkoṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻadevarkku yā
ṇṭu Irupattoṉpatāvatu taimmāsamutal tirukkaccūr tiruālakkoyil Uṭaiya nāyaṉārkkuc cantirātittavarai celvatāka Uttukkā
ṭṭuk koṭṭattut tamaṉūr nāṭṭu velūrk kumāraṉ cāttaṉ tiruvekampamuṭaiyāṉ makaṉ centāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku 2 Iraṇ
ṭukku Ikkoyiṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya civabrāhmaṇaṉ bhāradvāji kāñcikkuṟi śrīkaṇṭapaṭṭaṉ tevappiriyaṉ makaṉ Uṭaiyappiḷḷaiyāṉa nit
takalliyāṇa paṭṭaṉeṉ Ivaṉ pakkal nāṉ kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu 4 nāluṅkaikkoṇṭu Iccantiviḷakku Iraṇṭum cantirātittavarai E
rikka kaṭaveṉākac cammatittuc cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭutten Uṭaiya piḷḷaiyāṉa nittakalliyāṇapaṭṭaṉeṉ Itu paṉmāheśvararakṣai §
Digital edition of SII 26.317 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
